description: The Yaris is the ultimate urban companion, blending Toyota's world-leading hybrid technology with a compact footprint. Perfect for navigating tight city streets without compromising on fuel efficiency.
Don't let the size fool you. The Honda Jazz features 'Magic Seats' and a highly advanced e:HEV powertrain, offering a smooth, EV-like driving experience with class-leading interior space.
A cult classic known for its agile handling. The new Swift features a lightweight chassis and a mild hybrid system that makes every corner a joy to turn while keeping costs low.
The Cooper S is a premium powerhouse in a small package. With a 2.0L turbo engine, it delivers the iconic 'Go-Kart' feel that has made Mini a legend on both the track and the street.
Italian style meets a sustainable future. The 500e retains its retro charm while offering a zippy electric range and a tech-focused cabin designed for the modern fashionista.
Big on features, small in size. The i10 offers one of the most comprehensive safety packages in its class, making it the perfect choice for first-time buyers and city dwellers.
The Polo brings 'Big Car' refinement to the compact segment. Solid build quality, a quiet cabin, and the efficient TSI engine make it a sophisticated choice for any journey.
A blend of elegance and technology. The Clio E-Tech Hybrid uses Formula 1 inspired engineering to provide a responsive drive with incredible fuel savings.
The 208 stands out with its sharp 'saber' tooth LED lights and the immersive i-Cockpit. It's a high-tech hatchback that feels much more expensive than its price tag suggests.
Lexus brings Takumi craftsmanship to the small car segment. The LBX is a self-charging hybrid that defines luxury through silence, premium materials, and effortless city driving.

The 2025 Camry goes all-hybrid, combining a 2.5L engine with a powerful electric motor. It offers a whisper-quiet cabin and a redesigned 'Hammerhead' front fascia that brings a modern, aggressive edge to the world's favorite family sedan.

Redefining the compact sedan, the Civic offers a low, wide stance and an expansive glass area for superior visibility. The updated hybrid powertrain delivers instant torque while maintaining the legendary Civic handling.
The new 'Highland' refresh brings a quieter ride, premium materials, and a sleek aerodynamic profile. With no engine noise and industry-leading software, the Model 3 is a smartphone on wheels.
The 3 Series remains the gold standard for sport sedans. Featuring the BMW Curved Display and an athletic chassis, it balances daily executive comfort with the DNA of a true driver's car.
A 'Baby S-Class' in every sense. The C-Class features a vertical 11.9-inch touchscreen and a mild-hybrid engine that provides a boost of power for smooth overtaking and supreme highway refinement.
The A4 is the pinnacle of understated German engineering. With standard Quattro all-wheel drive and the Virtual Cockpit, it offers a secure, high-tech environment for all weather conditions.
With its futuristic 'Seamless Horizon' LED lamp and a curved panoramic display, the Sonata looks like a concept car brought to life. It offers premium features at a mid-range price point.
The K5 is for those who want to stand out. Its 'Z-line' LED lights and driver-centric cockpit make it one of the most exciting sedans on the market, backed by an industry-leading 10-year warranty.
Renowned for its library-quiet cabin and cloud-like ride quality. The ES 300h hybrid combines luxury materials with exceptional fuel economy, making it the perfect long-distance cruiser.
The S60 brings Scandinavian minimalism to the sedan world. With world-class seating ergonomics and Google built-in, it’s a car designed for human comfort and peace of mind.

The Sienna redefines the minivan with a standard hybrid powertrain and available AWD. It’s designed for modern adventures, featuring best-in-class fuel economy and a spacious, high-tech interior that feels more like a premium lounge than a van.

The Odyssey remains the benchmark for family-hauling dynamics. With its powerful V6 engine and 'Magic Slide' second-row seats, it offers the perfect balance of driving pleasure and ultimate passenger convenience.

The Sprinter is the ultimate canvas for business or luxury conversions. Engineered for durability and high-capacity hauling, it brings Mercedes-Benz refinement to the world of heavy-duty commercial transport.

The Ford Transit is the backbone of modern logistics. Highly customizable and incredibly reliable, it offers a car-like driving position with the massive volume required for serious cargo or passenger duties.

Rejecting the typical 'minivan' look, the Carnival brings SUV styling to the segment. It’s an 'MPV' that offers VIP Lounge seating and a massive panoramic dashboard for a truly premium family experience.

The T7 Multivan brings the legendary VW Bus into the digital age. Built on the MQB platform, it drives like a premium car while providing the flexibility needed for both professional work and active family lifestyles.

With its futuristic silhouette inspired by a spaceship, the Staria is a head-turner. Inside, it offers expansive glass for a 'panoramic' view and a luxurious, minimalist interior that redefines passenger comfort.
The ProMaster is built for efficiency with a front-wheel-drive system that allows for a lower load floor height. It’s a purposeful tool designed for maximum uptime and easy upfitting for any trade.

The Traveller is an invitation to travel in style. Its elegant design and high-end finishings make it the preferred choice for executive shuttles and large families who refuse to compromise on French elegance.

World-renowned for being indestructible, the HiAce is the global standard for reliable transport. The latest generation adds significant safety tech and improved comfort to its legendary workhorse status.

The icon of off-roading. The 2025 Wrangler features a heavy-duty full-float rear axle and the legendary Rock-Trac 4x4 system. With its removable doors and roof, it remains the ultimate open-air adventure vehicle for conquering the toughest terrain.

Blending 1960s heritage with modern tech, the Bronco is built to take you anywhere. Featuring G.O.A.T. (Goes Over Any Type of Terrain) modes and the Sasquatch package, it’s a high-speed desert runner and a technical rock crawler in one.

Small, light, and virtually unstoppable. The Jimny uses a ladder frame chassis and a 4WD system with low-range transfer gear to reach places that much larger vehicles cannot. It's the ultimate 'back-to-basics' off-road machine.

The Defender 110 redefines 21st-century adventure. It combines a highly durable 'unibody' architecture with luxury materials and advanced Terrain Response tech, making it as comfortable on the school run as it is crossing the Sahara.

The legend returns with a new hybrid heart. The 2025 Land Cruiser focuses on off-road agility and heritage design. Its i-FORCE MAX powertrain provides massive torque from low RPMs, perfect for crawling over technical obstacles.

The 'G-Wagon' remains an engineering marvel. For 2025, it gains a more efficient inline-six with electric assist. Despite its luxury status, with three locking differentials, it is still one of the most capable SUVs ever built.

The GX 550 is a 'sophisticated beast.' It combines Lexus luxury with a rugged body-on-frame construction. The Overtrail grade includes electronic kinetic dynamic suspension (E-KDDS) for maximum wheel articulation.

After a decade, the 4Runner is completely reborn. Built on the TNGA-F global truck platform, it features a roll-down rear window and incredible durability, ready to continue its legacy as a favorite for overlanders.

The only convertible truck in its class. The Gladiator takes the Wrangler's legendary 4x4 capability and adds a versatile 5-foot steel bed. It's the perfect tool for hauling gear to the furthest reaches of the map.
Known for its 'over-engineered' diesel engine, the D-Max is a workhorse that refuses to quit. The 3.0L turbo-diesel provides relentless torque for towing and traversing deep mud or water with ease.

The mid-engine revolution continues. The C8 Stingray delivers exotic-level performance at a fraction of the cost, featuring a driver-centric cockpit and a screaming naturally aspirated V8 that rivals European elite.

A bridge between the track-focused STO and the road-oriented EVO. The Tecnica offers rear-wheel-drive purity with advanced LDVI (Lamborghini Integrated Vehicle Dynamics) for a raw, visceral driving experience.
The 992.2 facelift brings the 911 into a digital era. With updated turbos from the previous GTS model and a fully digital cockpit, it remains the most versatile and precise sports car in the world.
The 2025 LCI update refines the M4 with new lighting signatures and a power bump for xDrive models. It's a high-performance athlete that masters the balance between daily comfort and track dominance.

The 7th generation S650 Mustang keeps the V8 soul alive. Featuring a dual-intake engine and an available electronic 'Drift Brake,' it combines old-school muscle with fighter-jet inspired interior tech.
The purist's choice. Lightweight, rear-wheel drive, and perfectly balanced. The GR86 is designed by Gazoo Racing to deliver maximum feedback and handling joy without needing supercar power.
Defining the 'fun to drive' concept. The 296 GTB pairs a V6 with a plug-in hybrid system for nearly 820 horsepower, delivering a sound so pure it's nicknamed the 'Piccolo handles like a dream.'
Mid-engine perfection. The 718 Cayman offers a level of communication between driver and road that is unmatched. It is arguably the best-handling platform in Porsche's current lineup.
A love letter to 50 years of Z heritage. With styling cues from the 240Z and 300ZX, the new Z features a twin-turbo V6 and a mechanical limited-slip differential for classic RWD thrills.
The world's best-selling roadster. The 2025 Miata focuses on weight reduction and human-machine unity. It's not about the speed; it's about the feeling of the wind and the perfect gear shift.

Widely considered the 'Best Car in the World' at its launch, the Silver Ghost set the standard for reliability and near-silent operation. Its over-engineered 7.4L engine was designed to run so smoothly that a coin could be balanced on the radiator while the engine was idling.

The car that put the world on wheels. Henry Ford's masterpiece utilized the first moving assembly line, making automobile ownership possible for the middle class. Rugged and simple, it could navigate the unpaved muddy roads of early 20th-century America with ease.

Born from a Le Mans-winning racer, the 300SL introduced the world to fuel injection and iconic 'Gullwing' doors. Its tubular spaceframe chassis necessitated the high sills that made the unique door design a mechanical necessity rather than just a stylistic choice.

Enzo Ferrari famously called the E-Type 'the most beautiful car ever made.' Beyond its looks, it offered 150 mph performance at a fraction of the cost of its Italian rivals, becoming the definitive symbol of the 'Swinging Sixties.'

Perhaps the most famous car in the world due to its association with James Bond. The DB5 is the quintessence of the British Grand Tourer, combining Italian Superleggera styling with a powerful 4.0L straight-six engine.

The 'Holy Grail' of car collecting. Only 36 were ever produced to compete in the FIA's Group 3 Grand Touring category. It represents the absolute pinnacle of the front-engine V12 era, dominating tracks worldwide in the early 60s.

Designed by Ferdinand Porsche, the Beetle became the best-selling car in history. Its air-cooled, rear-engine layout was designed for simplicity and longevity, becoming a cultural icon for generations of drivers.

The birth of a legend. Replacing the 356, the first 911 introduced the iconic six-cylinder 'boxer' engine. Its distinctive silhouette and rear-engine driving dynamics created a blueprint that Porsche still follows 60 years later.

The first American sports car. Featuring a fiberglass body and a 'Blue Flame' straight-six, the 1953 Corvette was a bold experiment by GM. While early models were cruisers, they paved the way for America's high-performance future.

Commonly known as the 'Goddess' (Déesse), the DS looked like it landed from another planet in 1955. It introduced hydropneumatic self-leveling suspension, providing a 'magic carpet' ride that no other car could match.

The RAV4 remains the world's most versatile compact SUV. With its hybrid efficiency and rugged 'Adventure' grade styling, it offers a balanced ride that transition effortlessly from city commutes to weekend trails.

Setting the bar for interior space and cargo flexibility, the CR-V features a clean, minimalist dashboard and Honda’s stable handling. It is widely considered the most user-friendly SUV for growing families.

The Tucson stands out with its 'Parametric Hidden Lights' and bold geometric body lines. Inside, the buttonless console and dual-stacked screens offer a futuristic environment rarely seen at this price point.

Combining German engineering with a sophisticated ride, the Tiguan is one of the few compact SUVs to offer an available third row. It’s the 'grown-up' choice for those who want a premium feel without the luxury price tag.
The Telluride has dominated the 3-row segment since its debut. It offers a near-luxury experience with high-end materials, massive passenger space, and a confident V6 engine that’s ready for towing.

The X5 continues to be the ultimate Sports Activity Vehicle. With a silky smooth inline-six and the latest BMW Curved Display, it provides an athletic driving experience that few other SUVs can replicate.
Practicality personified. The Highlander focuses on safety and longevity. Its turbocharged powertrain provides ample torque for highway merging even when fully loaded with eight passengers and gear.

The Cayenne is a sports car in an SUV’s body. Following a massive 2024 interior and chassis update, it delivers a driving feel that is characteristically Porsche, featuring incredible poise and high-speed stability.
An American icon of size and strength. The Tahoe is built on a truck-based frame, allowing for massive towing capacity and an interior large enough to serve as a mobile office or family headquarters.

The Sportage features a striking 'Opposites United' design philosophy. With class-leading rear legroom and a massive curved digital interface, it feels significantly more expensive than its sticker price suggests.
